Import TensorFlow (tf), TensorFlow Datasets (tfds), and Keras modules (keras, layers) required for model building and training
Import matplotlib functions (figure, subplot, imshow, axis, tight_layout, show, close) for image visualization
Set fixed batch size to 4 for training and validation datasets to match the error shape dimensions
Set image size to 64x64 and ensure dataset images are resized accordingly
Load the COCO or a compatible object detection dataset (e.g., 'coco/2017' or 'coco/2017', 'train' split) using tfds.load with shuffle_files=True
Preprocess dataset by normalizing images and preparing bounding boxes and class labels with fixed batch size 4; ensure bounding boxes vary between batches to mimic real detection data
Batch the dataset with batch_size=4 and drop_remainder=True to enforce consistent batch shapes
Instantiate the YOLOv8 model from keras_cv.models.YOLOv8 with default parameters and input_shape=(64,64,3)
Compile the YOLOv8 model with an appropriate optimizer (e.g., Adam) and detection losses as in the example
Define the training dataset iterator and validation dataset iterator with batch size 4 and proper preprocessing to output bounding boxes and labels compatible with YOLOv8 expectations

Start training the YOLOv8 model with yolo.fit for 3 epochs on the training dataset and validation dataset, passing a callback EvaluateCOCOMetricsCallback or equivalent that computes COCO metrics at epoch end
Monitor training output through the first epoch until the loss prints approximately as 'loss: 21.5977 - box_loss: 2.6112 - class_loss: 18.9865', matching the error log
Observe that upon epoch 1 completion, an UnknownError occurs during the EvaluateCOCOMetricsCallback's metrics.result(force=True) call
Verify the stack trace reports InvalidArgumentError in tf.concat operation with mismatched shape dimensions on axis=1, specifically shape[0] = [4,2,4] vs shape[1] = [4,1,4]
Assert that the error is reproducible consistently when running this training script on Colab Pro or equivalent environment with TensorFlow 2.x and KerasCV installed
Confirm that the error occurs during the metric evaluation step after the first epoch of training and not earlier during loss computation or forward passes
Optionally, print shapes of ground truth boxes passed to the metric callback to verify the dimension mismatch causing the concat failure
